title: Repairlets marketplace + evented task runner for support automation
preview:
Goal: Ship a lightweight marketplace for small, versioned remediation primitives ("repairlets") and an evented task runner that can invoke them safely from telemetry or manual triggers.

Scope:
- Define a repairlet schema (idempotent inputs, outputs, metadata, semantic tags, permissions) and a signing/versioning mod...
text:
Goal: Ship a lightweight marketplace for small, versioned remediation primitives ("repairlets") and an evented task runner that can invoke them safely from telemetry or manual triggers.

Scope:
- Define a repairlet schema (idempotent inputs, outputs, metadata, semantic tags, permissions) and a signing/versioning model suitable for marketplace consumption.
- Implement an evented task runner service that consumes a canonical event bus (alerts/telemetry/scheduled jobs) and maps events to repairlet invocations via rules and playbooks.
- Provide a UI surface in Console (browse, install, pin, and audit repairlets) + a lightweight API for programmatic invocation.
- Safety features: dry-run/simulate, RBAC gating, scoped secrets (short-lived tokens), automatic canary/rollback policies, and audit logs.
- Developer ergonomics: a CLI for packaging repairlets from runbook fragments, local dryrun h...

title: Avatar border and sparkline visibility in low-light theme
preview: Audit and implement token mapping and component overrides to improve perceived contrast and visual clarity for small surface components (avatar borders, compact controls, sparklines) in the low-light theme. Current issues: 1) 1px avatar borders disappear against deep surfaces at small sizes; 2) sparklines lose reada...
text: Audit and implement token mapping and component overrides to improve perceived contrast and visual clarity for small surface components (avatar borders, compact controls, sparklines) in the low-light theme. Current issues: 1) 1px avatar borders disappear against deep surfaces at small sizes; 2) sparklines lose readability when tokenized stroke colors are mapped directly from light theme swatches; 3) compact table row separators and dense control focus rings have inconsistent foreground priority. Deliverables: token adjustments, new fallback rules, component-level overrides for Avatar, Sparkline, TableRow, and CompactButton, automated visual tests, and a brief dev handoff document for runtime engineers on mapping rules and theming flags.

title: Create accessibility playbook and remediation plan for compact action pills, tokens, and modal focus
preview: Audit and remediate accessibility gaps for the compact action pill component family and related token surface (contrast tokens, disabled states, focus rings) and produce a reusable playbook and VPAT evidence package. Scope: compact and dense variants of pills, inline action buttons, small table row actions, and moda...
text: Audit and remediate accessibility gaps for the compact action pill component family and related token surface (contrast tokens, disabled states, focus rings) and produce a reusable playbook and VPAT evidence package. Scope: compact and dense variants of pills, inline action buttons, small table row actions, and modal focus behavior when those controls appear. Deliverables: 1) remediation checklist and actionable token mappings, 2) design + CSS examples and code snippets for focus/hover/pressed states, 3) regression test harness (Cypress + a11y snapshot tests), 4) VPAT evidence document with pass/fail artifacts and repro steps.

title: Warp-tiler slab-wrap and prefetch index-fence leading to KV overwrite and subtle numeric skew
preview: Summary: A concurrency bug in the warp-tiler prefetch path can cause an off-by-one slab wrap combined with an index-fence ordering violation. That results in KV slab slots being overwritten during concurrent prefill+decode windows. Symptoms include rare token duplication, subtle numerical drift in softmax accumulato...
text:
Summary: A concurrency bug in the warp-tiler prefetch path can cause an off-by-one slab wrap combined with an index-fence ordering violation. That results in KV slab slots being overwritten during concurrent prefill+decode windows. Symptoms include rare token duplication, subtle numerical drift in softmax accumulators under mixed-precision, and occasional transient degradation in prompt continuation quality.

Steps to reproduce (intermittent):
1) Start a dedicated workload with adaptive-kernel-routing enabled (--adaptive-kernel=true) on an 8-GPU pool and model variant 'redwood-open-16k-quant'.
2) Send a burst of interleaved prefill requests (batch_size=4, prefix lengths varying 512-1024) while simultaneously issuing low-latency decode continuations on other shards.
3) Observe error rate increase and rerun with debug KV instrumentation enabled (RUNTIME_KV_TRACE=1) to capture slab indic...

title: Telemetry-first streaming chat handler and retry ergonomics for Python SDK
preview: Problem: Current Python streaming chat helpers focus on developer ergonomics for event dispatch but lack integrated telemetry hooks and consistent retry/cancellation semantics. Teams building conversational features need: (1) deterministic event ordering and checkpointing for resumability, (2) lightweight telemetry ...
text:
Problem: Current Python streaming chat helpers focus on developer ergonomics for event dispatch but lack integrated telemetry hooks and consistent retry/cancellation semantics. Teams building conversational features need: (1) deterministic event ordering and checkpointing for resumability, (2) lightweight telemetry (latency/cost/seen-events) surfaced per-chat and per-token, and (3) simple but correct retry semantics that work with partial responses and idempotent handler code.

Goal: Ship a telemetry-first handler primitive and a short set of patterns/examples that make it easy for app teams to implement robust stream consumers that: resume cleanly after transient failures, emit standardized telemetry points, tolerate duplicates, and surface developer-level signals for graceful cancellation and backpressure.

This ticket delivers: API prototypes, a conservative default implementation ...

title: Admin Action Custody Flow: Data Residency Locking, Retention Policies, and Append-Only Exporter
preview: Deliver an append-only export pipeline and residency-aware custody flow for admin actions (config changes, role grants, access revocations). This ticket covers: (1) a write-once exporter that pushes signed batches into region-scoped WORM buckets, (2) manifest generation with cryptographic custody stamps and retentio...
text: Deliver an append-only export pipeline and residency-aware custody flow for admin actions (config changes, role grants, access revocations). This ticket covers: (1) a write-once exporter that pushes signed batches into region-scoped WORM buckets, (2) manifest generation with cryptographic custody stamps and retention markers, and (3) an automated evidence report that exports compliance-ready bundles for legal/forensic requests. Goal: ensure tamper-evident admin audit data with programmable retention and easy handoff for auditors.

title: Eval runbook for automated batching/caching configuration changes
preview: Short summary: Create a reproducible evaluation runbook and automation to validate quality regressions introduced by automated batching and KV-caching recommendations. The goal is to be able to safely apply cost-saving configurations at scale with automated acceptance checks and deterministic rollback triggers.
text: Short summary: Create a reproducible evaluation runbook and automation to validate quality regressions introduced by automated batching and KV-caching recommendations. The goal is to be able to safely apply cost-saving configurations at scale with automated acceptance checks and deterministic rollback triggers.

title: Cross-SDK resilience guidelines and ergonomic examples for retry/backoff and idempotency
preview: Purpose: define a single, pragmatic set of defaults and ergonomic primitives for retry/backoff behavior and idempotency handling across Redwood SDKs (Python/TypeScript/Go), plus a clear taxonomy of retryable errors and streaming-specific guidance. Goals: (1) reduce customer error-handling copy/paste, (2) make safe d...
text:
Purpose: define a single, pragmatic set of defaults and ergonomic primitives for retry/backoff behavior and idempotency handling across Redwood SDKs (Python/TypeScript/Go), plus a clear taxonomy of retryable errors and streaming-specific guidance. Goals: (1) reduce customer error-handling copy/paste, (2) make safe defaults that work for most production workloads, (3) provide escape hatches and migration guidance for advanced cases, (4) ensure consistent telemetry and tests across SDKs.

Scope: client-side SDK behaviors for synchronous requests, async/promises, and streaming APIs. Does NOT change server-side semantics except where noted (server may choose to enforce idempotency-key dedup semantics).

Non-goals: mandate server-side idempotency enforcement, replace application-level business idempotency logic, or enforce a single header name for every protocol variation (we recommend but...

title: Helm release stuck after operator upgrade — incident response, config drift reconciliation, and customer hotfix deployment
preview: Several customers running Private (VPC/on-prem) reported failed operator upgrades where Helm remained in a 'pending-upgrade' or 'failed-hook' state and the operator reconciliation loop stopped applying subsequent changes. Symptoms observed across multiple clusters: helm release pending due to a pre/post upgrade hook...
text: Several customers running Private (VPC/on-prem) reported failed operator upgrades where Helm remained in a 'pending-upgrade' or 'failed-hook' state and the operator reconciliation loop stopped applying subsequent changes. Symptoms observed across multiple clusters: helm release pending due to a pre/post upgrade hook that never completed, operator pod entering CrashLoopBackOff after CRD validation errors, and configmaps/opaque secrets diverging between control plane intent and node-level state. Impact: customer-facing control plane features degraded, unable to deploy model revisions or scale worker pools. This ticket coordinates incident response, manual hotfix deployments for affected customers, and a durable fix to Helm/operator upgrade flow to prevent config drift and stuck releases.

title: SLO-driven token-budget scheduler with KV slab isolation
preview: Goal: implement an SLO-aware scheduling layer for the continuous batcher that enforces per-customer and global latency SLOs via an explicit token-budget preemption model and a KV cache slab isolation scheme to reduce costly cross-tenant KV eviction. This ticket covers design, implementation, benchmarks, and canary r...
text: Goal: implement an SLO-aware scheduling layer for the continuous batcher that enforces per-customer and global latency SLOs via an explicit token-budget preemption model and a KV cache slab isolation scheme to reduce costly cross-tenant KV eviction. This ticket covers design, implementation, benchmarks, and canary rollout of: (1) a token-budget allocator that gives each inflight request a consumable token budget representing GPU memory + compute entitlement for a sliding window; (2) a preemption mechanism that uses the token budget to decide cooperative vs. forced preemption under contention; and (3) KV cache slab partitioning by tenant/priority band to bound worst-case memory amplification from large KV caches. Motivation: we have observed tail latency spikes during mixed workloads (long-context completions + many small chat requests) where continuous batching saturates GPU memory an...

title: APIs accepting rotated API keys due to proxy acceptance-window off-by-one
preview: Production customers reported intermittent 401/200 anomalies after a scheduled API key rotation. Investigation found an acceptance-window off-by-one bug in egress proxy token validation that allowed some rotated API keys to be accepted for ~30s after rotation, and in other cases caused freshly rotated keys to be rej...
text: Production customers reported intermittent 401/200 anomalies after a scheduled API key rotation. Investigation found an acceptance-window off-by-one bug in egress proxy token validation that allowed some rotated API keys to be accepted for ~30s after rotation, and in other cases caused freshly rotated keys to be rejected. This ticket tracks the hotfix, rollout, and follow-ups across Hosted/Dedicated/Private deployments.

title: Proportional KV Cache Allocation and Pipe-Aware Scheduling for Multi-GPU Inference
preview: Design and implement a proportional KV cache allocation algorithm and pipeline-aware scheduling layer to optimize multi-GPU memory utilization for continuous-batching inference. The goal is to reduce token-level memory pressure and tail latency variance when models are served across mixed tensor/pipeline parallel to...
text: Design and implement a proportional KV cache allocation algorithm and pipeline-aware scheduling layer to optimize multi-GPU memory utilization for continuous-batching inference. The goal is to reduce token-level memory pressure and tail latency variance when models are served across mixed tensor/pipeline parallel topologies. Deliverables: design doc, prototype scheduler integrated with runtime, microbenchmarks showing memory and latency improvements, and a rollout plan for Dedicated clusters.

title: Heterogeneous phase allocation policy with attention-pressure mitigation
preview:
Background:
The serving runtime currently uses a monolithic phase-splitting approach for prefill and decode that assumes homogeneous attention and kernel costs across requests. As we onboard more heterogeneous models and tenant mixes (short-chat vs. long-context summarization), we observe increased tail latency due ...
text:
Background:
The serving runtime currently uses a monolithic phase-splitting approach for prefill and decode that assumes homogeneous attention and kernel costs across requests. As we onboard more heterogeneous models and tenant mixes (short-chat vs. long-context summarization), we observe increased tail latency due to attention hotspots and suboptimal kernel-selection choices when slices contain mixed-cost segments.

Problem statement:
- Tenant fairness and latency SLOs are drifting during microbursts from tenants with few long-context requests.
- Kernel selection heuristics are brittle when a microbatch contains a mix of low-attention and high-attention sequences; this increases overall token cost and latency variance.
- Current scheduler cannot express tenant-level latency contracts without sacrificing throughput for the entire worker pool.

Goals:
1) Design a phase allocation polic...

title: Slotted-retirement policy for GPU KV cache: bounded-staleness with prefix integrity
preview:
Problem:
When we mix TTL-driven expiration, LRU pressure and prefix-affine allocations on the GPU, loose eviction (per-key) leads to two classes of production correctness issues: 1) prefix-splitting where a prefix's KV history is partially evicted causing reranked completions to return inconsistent context, and 2) i...
text:
Problem:
When we mix TTL-driven expiration, LRU pressure and prefix-affine allocations on the GPU, loose eviction (per-key) leads to two classes of production correctness issues: 1) prefix-splitting where a prefix's KV history is partially evicted causing reranked completions to return inconsistent context, and 2) in-flight batch regressions where a request references a KV slot that is reclaimed mid-batch. Both are exacerbated by continuous batching (variable-length micro-batches) and GPU memory compaction strategies.

Goal:
Design and implement a low-latency, low-cost GPU KV reclamation policy that: (a) bounds staleness deterministically (an upper bound on how old a referenced KV may be), (b) preserves prefix-level integrity (no partial prefix retirements visible to inference), (c) cooperates with continuous batching to avoid mid-batch invalidation, and (d) enables efficient bulk rec...

title: Dynamic padding-squeeze and bucket fusion for runtime micro-batching
preview: Reduce padding-induced waste and improve GPU utilization by implementing a runtime component that squeezes variable-length sequences into tight micro-batches using dynamic bucketing and bucket fusion across prefill and decode stages. The feature should work with existing KV-cache and fallback gracefully for latency-...
text: Reduce padding-induced waste and improve GPU utilization by implementing a runtime component that squeezes variable-length sequences into tight micro-batches using dynamic bucketing and bucket fusion across prefill and decode stages. The feature should work with existing KV-cache and fallback gracefully for latency-sensitive requests.

title: Observability Governor service: enforce scrub, retention, and access-audit at ingest
preview:
Problem: telemetry streams (traces, logs, metrics) currently apply inconsistent PII redaction and retention rules across SDKs and ingestion pipelines. That leads to support exposures, divergent retention behavior per tenant, and difficulty proving compliance.

Proposal: build an "Observability Governor" service that...
text:
Problem: telemetry streams (traces, logs, metrics) currently apply inconsistent PII redaction and retention rules across SDKs and ingestion pipelines. That leads to support exposures, divergent retention behavior per tenant, and difficulty proving compliance.

Proposal: build an "Observability Governor" service that enforces telemetry governance decisions at ingest time: deterministic pseudonymization / tokenization, field-level scrubbing (regex + schema-aware), tenant-scoped retention enforcement, and mandatory RBAC access-audit logging. The Governor will be policy-driven (runtime rules), pluggable into the existing ingestion path (as a lightweight microservice in the dataplane with sidecar option), and expose admin APIs for policy lifecycle and data erasure requests. It will also provide SDK hooks so producers can surface structured annotations and opt into safe-debug tokens.

Goals...

title: Proactive schema sentinel for config & model rollout gating
preview: Create a lightweight, automated "sentinel" system that detects quality regressions in structured outputs (JSON/typed blobs) tied to configuration or model changes and blocks or annotates rollouts. The sentinel will run a compact eval suite against a golden anchor dataset and live traffic simulated inputs, compute pe...
text: Create a lightweight, automated "sentinel" system that detects quality regressions in structured outputs (JSON/typed blobs) tied to configuration or model changes and blocks or annotates rollouts. The sentinel will run a compact eval suite against a golden anchor dataset and live traffic simulated inputs, compute per-field integrity and semantic deltas, and expose pass/fail signals into the rollout engine so canary percentages can be gated automatically.

title: Sliding-window KV tiling memory profiles for Llama/Mistral/Qwen long-context variants
preview: Goal: produce per-model, sliding-window ‘tiling’ memory profiles that reduce peak GPU working set for long-context Llama/Mistral/Qwen variants while preserving continuous-batching throughput and KV cache hit-rate. This ticket explores a tiling strategy where KV residency is managed as overlapping tiles aligned to ty...
text: Goal: produce per-model, sliding-window ‘tiling’ memory profiles that reduce peak GPU working set for long-context Llama/Mistral/Qwen variants while preserving continuous-batching throughput and KV cache hit-rate. This ticket explores a tiling strategy where KV residency is managed as overlapping tiles aligned to typical sequence windows (e.g., 2k/4k/16k), with soft eviction boundaries to minimize re-population cost under interleaved batching.

title: SLO lease transfers and graceful traffic atrophy policy for model endpoints
preview: Context: Redwood runs model endpoints across regions and model variants with different cost/latency characteristics. Teams have expressed the need for a formal mechanism to temporarily 'lease' SLO headroom from lower-priority routes/variants to keep customer-critical routes online during short spikes, then return or...
text: Context: Redwood runs model endpoints across regions and model variants with different cost/latency characteristics. Teams have expressed the need for a formal mechanism to temporarily 'lease' SLO headroom from lower-priority routes/variants to keep customer-critical routes online during short spikes, then return or rebalance that lease without causing cascading failures. This ticket defines a policy and operational plan for SLO lease transfers and a complementary 'graceful traffic atrophy' (GTA) strategy that intentionally sheds low-value traffic in an observable, reversible way.

title: Schema decorator + runtime coercion and humanized diagnostics for tool calls
preview: Introduce a JSON-schema ‘decorator’ layer and a runtime coercion/partial-validation path for function/tool calls. Goal: reduce class of user-facing errors without sacrificing deterministic behavior for production workloads. Deliver a compact decorator format (x-*) that augments standard JSON Schema with compatibilit...
text: Introduce a JSON-schema ‘decorator’ layer and a runtime coercion/partial-validation path for function/tool calls. Goal: reduce class of user-facing errors without sacrificing deterministic behavior for production workloads. Deliver a compact decorator format (x-*) that augments standard JSON Schema with compatibility and UX hints, implement a permissive-but-visible validation pass that returns structured diagnostics, and expose SDK-friendly error mappings and suggestions. Also add streaming validation for very large payloads and compatibility shims for legacy tool manifests.

title: Localized keyboard focus order and rollout-toast contrast audit for dashboards
preview: Problem: Several dashboard controls (timeline scrubs, per-route rollout toggles, and ephemeral rollout toasts) generate dynamic DOM order and visual-only focus styles that break keyboard navigation and screenreader announcements in non-English locales and RTL layouts. Color tokens used for toast backgrounds and time...
text:
Problem: Several dashboard controls (timeline scrubs, per-route rollout toggles, and ephemeral rollout toasts) generate dynamic DOM order and visual-only focus styles that break keyboard navigation and screenreader announcements in non-English locales and RTL layouts. Color tokens used for toast backgrounds and timeline scrims fall below WCAG 2.1 AA contrast thresholds against localized number/label color variants.

Goal: Produce a design audit and prototype that ensures: keyboard focus order is predictable and stable across localized label length changes and RTL; toast and scrim color tokens meet contrast ratios with localized text and semantic overlays; screenreader announcements reflect current rollout state changes without duplicating messages when language changes are applied.

Acceptance criteria:
- Deterministic tab/focus order for dashboard widgets and rollout controls that do...

title: Scoutboard: Concise Failure Hierarchy & Snapshot Quickflows
preview: Design a compact, glancable console surface (Scoutboard) that surfaces a hierarchical summary of failures across models/routes and provides one-tap snapshot quickflows to inspect payloads, trigger safe replays, or open a focused payload diff. The goal is an at-a-glance taxonomy for on-call and SREs that reduces fric...
text: Design a compact, glancable console surface (Scoutboard) that surfaces a hierarchical summary of failures across models/routes and provides one-tap snapshot quickflows to inspect payloads, trigger safe replays, or open a focused payload diff. The goal is an at-a-glance taxonomy for on-call and SREs that reduces friction when triaging noisy error spikes and enables safe, time-boxed exploration without leaving the dashboard.

title: Zero-downtime embedding dimension migration + client compatibility shim
preview:
Context:
We need to change the canonical dimensionality of text embeddings used by the reranking endpoint from 768 -> 1024 to support a new model family and improved quality. This ticket captures the migration plan, compatibility shims, cost/latency trade-offs, and rollout phases for zero-downtime reindexing and cli...
text:
Context:
We need to change the canonical dimensionality of text embeddings used by the reranking endpoint from 768 -> 1024 to support a new model family and improved quality. This ticket captures the migration plan, compatibility shims, cost/latency trade-offs, and rollout phases for zero-downtime reindexing and client impact mitigation.

Problem statement:
- Some customers embed and store vectors in customer indexes and call rerank endpoints that expect 768-dim vectors. Changing dims will break clients that send old vectors or clients that expect the API to return 768-dim vectors.
- Reindexing existing datasets is expensive and can cause prolonged degraded performance if done naively.

Goals:
1) Allow the backend to serve 1024-dim embeddings while remaining compatible with clients that still produce or expect 768-dim vectors.
2) Provide clear guidance and tooling for customers to rei...

title: Backcompat contracts and automated rollout validators across runtime, API, SDKs, Console
preview: Goal: Define machine-readable backwards-compatibility contracts per product surface (runtime, public API, SDKs, Console) and implement a set of automated rollout validators that run as pre-flight checks in CI and as runtime canary guards. This ticket covers the contract schema, the validator rules, a CLI pre-flight ...
text: Goal: Define machine-readable backwards-compatibility contracts per product surface (runtime, public API, SDKs, Console) and implement a set of automated rollout validators that run as pre-flight checks in CI and as runtime canary guards. This ticket covers the contract schema, the validator rules, a CLI pre-flight tool (redwood-upgrade-check), and CI/console integration for gating upgrades on Dedicated/Private/Hosted channels.

title: Safe automated batching/caching config rollouts with eval-driven golden-prompt acceptance
preview:
Background: We want to automatically recommend and apply batching/caching configuration changes to reduce per-token cost while avoiding quality regressions. Past attempts showed cost wins but introduced subtle output regressions for specific prompt patterns (notably multi-turn contexts and rare-entity prompts).

Goa...
text:
Background: We want to automatically recommend and apply batching/caching configuration changes to reduce per-token cost while avoiding quality regressions. Past attempts showed cost wins but introduced subtle output regressions for specific prompt patterns (notably multi-turn contexts and rare-entity prompts).

Goal: Implement an automated rollout pipeline that: (1) generates candidate batching/cache config changes, (2) validates them against a focused eval suite driven by golden prompts and task-specific metrics, (3) safely applies changes via staged rollouts (canary -> ramp) with automated rollback if acceptance thresholds fail, and (4) provides observability and post-mortem artifacts.

Scope: This ticket covers the eval gating system, golden prompt set curation, acceptance thresholds, canary spec, rollback runbook, and dashboarding hooks. Separate tickets will handle the UI in Con...

title: Soft-anchored capacity allocator for geo-failover routing
preview: Purpose: implement a soft-anchored capacity allocator that combines latency-first routing with per-region capacity credits and probabilistic spillover to preserve SLOs during partial region degradation or failover events. The allocator should: (1) prefer geo-pinned endpoints when latency and credits permit; (2) grad...
text:
Purpose: implement a soft-anchored capacity allocator that combines latency-first routing with per-region capacity credits and probabilistic spillover to preserve SLOs during partial region degradation or failover events. The allocator should: (1) prefer geo-pinned endpoints when latency and credits permit; (2) gradually shift traffic away from an impacted region using a probabilistic hedging factor rather than an all-or-nothing cutover; (3) enforce circuit-breaker style backpressure and per-customer soft quotas to avoid overload on receiving regions; and (4) expose observability and controls in Console for operators to tune hedging fraction, credit refill rates, and emergency evacuation overrides.

Why: full hard failover leads to large tail spikes and cross-region overload (thundering herd) when an upstream region becomes degraded. A soft, capacity-aware approach reduces tail latenc...

title: Audit evidence packaging API and residency-change notification webhooks
preview: Goal: Provide a single API and webhook surface that automates packaging and delivery of compliance evidence (audit logs, policy evaluations, retention actions) and notifies customers of data residency changes. The solution must: 1) produce tamper-evident evidence bundles (signed + SHA256) that can be retrieved or pu...
text:
Goal: Provide a single API and webhook surface that automates packaging and delivery of compliance evidence (audit logs, policy evaluations, retention actions) and notifies customers of data residency changes. The solution must: 1) produce tamper-evident evidence bundles (signed + SHA256) that can be retrieved or pushed to customer endpoints; 2) deliver residency-change events that include previous and target region metadata; 3) expose retention enforcement events with links to evidence artifacts; 4) support robust webhook delivery semantics (idempotency, retries, signature verification, batching).

API / Design summary:
- Evidence packaging API: POST /v1/compliance/evidence-generate
 - Request: {customer_id, timeframe_start, timeframe_end, include_logs: boolean, include_prompts: boolean, retention_policy_id}
 - Response: {evidence_id, status: queued|ready|failed, estimated_size_bytes...

title: Smart Batch Orchestrator: workload signatures, safety guards, and continuous window tuning
preview: Build a Smart Batch Orchestrator (SBO) that continuously recommends and safely applies batching and caching parameters per workload. SBO will profile live traffic to infer 'workload signatures' (inference footprint distribution, token length histograms, client concurrency patterns, and prompt prefix commonalities) a...
text: Build a Smart Batch Orchestrator (SBO) that continuously recommends and safely applies batching and caching parameters per workload. SBO will profile live traffic to infer 'workload signatures' (inference footprint distribution, token length histograms, client concurrency patterns, and prompt prefix commonalities) and produce a ranked set of parameter changes (batch size, aggregation window, token-threshold, aggressive KV cache retention) together with an estimated cost delta and latency risk. The ticket covers design and a first-pass implementation for: (1) offline profiling + feature extraction, (2) a heuristic recommender that maps signatures to parameter candidates, (3) safe-apply primitives (canary split, dry-run simulation, rollback triggers), and (4) Console UI hooks for review and acceptance. The goal is automated, conservative recommendations that reduce per-token cost withou...

title: Transient capacity cushion and billing smoothing policy for burst events
preview: Problem: Customers on Dedicated frequently experience short-lived traffic spikes (minutes) that exceed reserved throughput. Current behaviors either (a) allow immediate overage invoicing with large cost spikes or (b) fail requests when autoscale is slow. Goal: introduce a transient capacity cushion (TCC) and a billi...
text:
Problem: Customers on Dedicated frequently experience short-lived traffic spikes (minutes) that exceed reserved throughput. Current behaviors either (a) allow immediate overage invoicing with large cost spikes or (b) fail requests when autoscale is slow. Goal: introduce a transient capacity cushion (TCC) and a billing smoothing policy that provide predictable short-term burst handling without permanent changes to reserved capacity or surprising invoices.

Scope: design, server/runtime changes, billing pipeline adjustments, console UI, and operator controls. This ticket covers engineering design and initial implementation for the TCC core runtime and billing smoothing hook; downstream UI and docs tasks will be split as follow-ups.

Non-goals: permanent reservation changes, pricing redesign for long-term overages (>72 hours), or self-serve purchase flows for burst bundles (separate PM w...

title: Recency-aware length-bucketed sampler for per-tenant calibration packs
preview: Goal: Define and implement a reproducible sampling strategy to generate per-tenant (per-domain) calibration packs for FP8/INT8 quantization validation. The sampler must preserve production distribution properties relevant to inference quality while keeping pack sizes bounded for repeatable calibration runs. Key cons...
text: Goal: Define and implement a reproducible sampling strategy to generate per-tenant (per-domain) calibration packs for FP8/INT8 quantization validation. The sampler must preserve production distribution properties relevant to inference quality while keeping pack sizes bounded for repeatable calibration runs. Key constraints: (1) production prompt traffic is heavy-tailed across intents and tenants, (2) KV-cache and sequence length distributions affect quantization error, (3) datasets must be de-duped and privacy-safe, and (4) we need deterministic seeds and manifest metadata for later auditing.

title: Spiral rollout orchestration with probe-matrix checks and fallthrough routing lanes
preview: Goal: implement a 'spiral' rollout primitive that layers A/B slices, shadow probes, and progressive traffic promotion with deterministic fallthrough lanes by region and capacity tier. The spiral flow exercises a probe-matrix for compatibility checks (input shapes, output schema, kv-cache parity, streaming semantics)...
text:
Goal: implement a 'spiral' rollout primitive that layers A/B slices, shadow probes, and progressive traffic promotion with deterministic fallthrough lanes by region and capacity tier. The spiral flow exercises a probe-matrix for compatibility checks (input shapes, output schema, kv-cache parity, streaming semantics) before live traffic promotion. Fallthrough lanes define ordered routing fallback: local warm-variant -> regional-compatible-variant -> low-cost-quantized-variant -> hosted-fallback. We want deterministic, auditable routing decisions and clear automatic rollback triggers based on end-to-end quality signals and capacity SLO breaches.

Scope: routing policy composer, probe-matrix evaluation engine, fallthrough lane config schema, rollout controller changes, metric hooks for quality SLOs, canary traffic UI in Console, and docs for on-call. Excludes long-term rollout analytics ...

title: Invocation shims for cross-SDK function/tool calls with retry idempotency and streaming ergonomics
preview: Implement language idiomatic invocation shims across Python/TypeScript/Go SDKs that unify function/tool schema validation, partial/streaming function responses, and retry-safe invocation semantics. Goal is to provide: (1) a lightweight FunctionInvoker helper in each SDK that coerces and validates user-supplied argum...
text: Implement language idiomatic invocation shims across Python/TypeScript/Go SDKs that unify function/tool schema validation, partial/streaming function responses, and retry-safe invocation semantics. Goal is to provide: (1) a lightweight FunctionInvoker helper in each SDK that coerces and validates user-supplied arguments against model-declared schemas, (2) idempotency and dedup tokens to make retries safe across network failures and streaming interruptions, and (3) ergonomic streaming APIs that surface partial results and function invocation progress with minimal boilerplate. This ticket covers design choices, cross-language examples, and initial implementation for the Python and TypeScript SDKs with an engineering spike for Go ergonomics.

title: Tenant slot autoscaler + soft-QoS shaper for embeddings ingress
preview: Goal: Reduce tail latency and increase predictable throughput for the embeddings ingestion pipeline by introducing a tenant-slot based autoscaler and a soft-QoS shaper that gracefully degrades non-critical requests during congestion. Scope includes controller design, ingress admission changes, metrics, and rollout p...
text:
Goal: Reduce tail latency and increase predictable throughput for the embeddings ingestion pipeline by introducing a tenant-slot based autoscaler and a soft-QoS shaper that gracefully degrades non-critical requests during congestion. Scope includes controller design, ingress admission changes, metrics, and rollout plan.

Background: Current embedding endpoints suffer unpredictable p99 increases under multi-tenant bursts and have limited tenant-level controls. Existing work focuses on queue smoothing and provisioner strategies; this ticket targets an ingress-focused solution that prevents single-tenant bursts from destabilizing global pools while preserving throughput and quality for high-priority traffic.

Proposed solution:
- Implement a tenant-slot abstraction: each tenant is allocated a number of concurrency slots that map to admission tokens at the ingress router. Slots are elasti...

title: cold-kv-sojourn-eviction-watermark-probe-and-graceful-preempt-framework
preview: Introduce a conservative, low-latency preemption and fairness framework for continuous batching that explicitly accounts for KV cache residency (hot vs cold sojourns) and GPU memory fragmentation. Goals: reduce tail latency regressions for high-priority tenants, bound customer impact when eviction is necessary, and ...
text: Introduce a conservative, low-latency preemption and fairness framework for continuous batching that explicitly accounts for KV cache residency (hot vs cold sojourns) and GPU memory fragmentation. Goals: reduce tail latency regressions for high-priority tenants, bound customer impact when eviction is necessary, and avoid repeated cold-start churn for recently accessed KV entries.

title: Sequence Slab Prioritizer & Padding Trimmer for Prefill/Decode
preview: Goal: reduce padded tokens across prefill+decode micro-batches while preserving per-request P50 latency SLO and avoiding kernel thrashing. We propose a Sequence Slab Prioritizer that groups requests into compact 'slabs' by seq-len quantiles and kernel-affinity hints, combines a lightweight prefix coalescer for short...
text:
Goal: reduce padded tokens across prefill+decode micro-batches while preserving per-request P50 latency SLO and avoiding kernel thrashing. We propose a Sequence Slab Prioritizer that groups requests into compact 'slabs' by seq-len quantiles and kernel-affinity hints, combines a lightweight prefix coalescer for short-prefill-heavy traffic, and a pad-trimmer pass that opportunistically strips tail padding during GPU kernel invocation. Success metrics: 20-35% padded-token reduction in mixed production traffic, <=5% regression on P50 latency for smallest tier, and 0 net increase in kernel switch-count per second.

Design highlights:
- Quantile slab construction: calculate online seqlen quantiles (16/32/64/128 buckets) per 1s window and form slabs that balance token volume vs slab-count.
- Kernel affinity hints: annotate slabs with preferred kernel families (FP16/INT8/FlashAttention varian...

title: Dynamic memory cadence for shared GPU safety
preview: Context: Serving runtime must allocate GPU memory across model weights, KV cache, and activations while supporting continuous batching. Current static headroom allocations cause either wasted reserved memory or frequent OOMs during bursty traffic. Goal: implement a time-cadenced headroom recalculation and transactio...
text:
Context: Serving runtime must allocate GPU memory across model weights, KV cache, and activations while supporting continuous batching. Current static headroom allocations cause either wasted reserved memory or frequent OOMs during bursty traffic. Goal: implement a time-cadenced headroom recalculation and transactional per-tenant soft budgets that reconcile with the KV eviction/aging policy to improve utilization and reduce OOM rate.

Scope: algorithm and runtime changes in the scheduler and memory allocator; telemetry and SLOs; migration plan for rolling changes across running hosts. Not in scope: model weight compression (separate project).

title: Admission Moderator: resilience for quota controller outages, burst pacing and multi-region failover
preview: Problem statement: Recent incidents show our central quota controller can become a single point of pain during regional network partitions and flash bursts. When the limiter fabric loses the lease or RPCs stall, customers observe long tail latencies, opaque 429 spikes, and cascading retries that violate token-level ...
text: Problem statement: Recent incidents show our central quota controller can become a single point of pain during regional network partitions and flash bursts. When the limiter fabric loses the lease or RPCs stall, customers observe long tail latencies, opaque 429 spikes, and cascading retries that violate token-level SLOs. This ticket designs and implements an Admission Moderator layer that: 1) provides degraded but fair admission when quota store is partially unavailable, 2) enforces burst pacing to avoid downstream stalls, 3) integrates with multi-region circuit-breakers and prioritized shedding policies, and 4) emits telemetry needed for SLO guardrails and automated rollback triggers.

title: Concilium: token-coalescer for quantized kernel alignment
preview: Implement a token-coalescing runtime layer (Concilium) that reorganizes prefills and microbatches to maximize reuse for INT8/FP8-friendly blocked kernels. The goal is to reduce dequantization overhead and improve kernel alignment for attention and KV prefill paths without changing model semantics. Concilium will: 1)...
text: Implement a token-coalescing runtime layer (Concilium) that reorganizes prefills and microbatches to maximize reuse for INT8/FP8-friendly blocked kernels. The goal is to reduce dequantization overhead and improve kernel alignment for attention and KV prefill paths without changing model semantics. Concilium will: 1) coalesce adjacent token slices across streams where safe, 2) introduce a headwise remap metadata layer so blocked kernels can operate on a coalesced memory layout, 3) provide lazy dequantization views to avoid materializing full FP tensors when not needed, and 4) coordinate prefill scheduling so that coalesced blocks are filled and decoded with minimal synchronization overhead.

title: Service degradation catalog and incident autopsy canvas
preview: Goal: Create a compact, actionable catalog of service degradation signatures and a linked visual "autopsy canvas" that lets on-call and SRE quickly map from symptom -> telemetry -> probable root cause. This ticket covers taxonomy definition, schema for trace/log enrichment, required metric counters, and three Grafan...
text:
Goal: Create a compact, actionable catalog of service degradation signatures and a linked visual "autopsy canvas" that lets on-call and SRE quickly map from symptom -> telemetry -> probable root cause. This ticket covers taxonomy definition, schema for trace/log enrichment, required metric counters, and three Grafana dashboards (Degradation Map, Error Slice Explorer, Incident Autopsy Canvas).

Scope:
- Define failure signatures for timeouts, overloaded workers, model-level failures (OOM, decode errors, generation truncation), HTTP/gRPC codes and proxies, client-side aborts, and retry-induced amplification.
- Propose standardized trace/log fields and enum values (e.g. redwood.error_class, redwood.model_status, redwood.slo_bucket) and a migration plan for instrumented services.
- Implement tracing hooks in runtime that add fields when failures are observed and propagate trace_id through...

title: Golden-output test harness for typed SDK responses and streaming retry semantics
preview: Create a reproducible 'golden output' test harness for the Python SDK that exercises typed response models (pydantic + dataclasses), streaming chunk reassembly, and retry/idempotency behaviors. The harness should produce deterministic fixtures that CI can validate across platforms and Python versions, provide a pyte...
text: Create a reproducible 'golden output' test harness for the Python SDK that exercises typed response models (pydantic + dataclasses), streaming chunk reassembly, and retry/idempotency behaviors. The harness should produce deterministic fixtures that CI can validate across platforms and Python versions, provide a pytest plugin for test ergonomics, and include example end-to-end tests that simulate transient network errors, backpressure, and resumed streams with checkpoints. This will help prevent regressions in structured output parsing, streaming hydration, and retry policies when changing SDK parsing logic or runtime semantics.

title: Graceful rate-limit throttling and credit-repair for active streaming sessions
preview:
Summary:

Active streaming sessions that encounter server-side rate-limiting (429s) or abrupt in-flight throttles can produce inconsistent billing outcomes: clients may be recharged for provisional token credits, or conversely, fail to receive credit for fragments that were generated but never finalized. This ticket...
text:
Summary:

Active streaming sessions that encounter server-side rate-limiting (429s) or abrupt in-flight throttles can produce inconsistent billing outcomes: clients may be recharged for provisional token credits, or conversely, fail to receive credit for fragments that were generated but never finalized. This ticket implements a conservative, auditable mechanism to (1) throttle streams gracefully to avoid orphan charges, (2) track provisional credits during throttles, and (3) perform automated credit-repair/reconciliation when sessions either resume or are abandoned.

Problem details and motivators:
- Observed during high-concurrency incident on 2026-02-28: when the regional rate limiter injected backpressure, ~0.7% of active streaming sessions ended in a midstream 429 followed by client retry. Billing ledger showed either double-charges (provisional + final) or lost credits depending...

title: Operational Templates: parameterized admin workflows + automation harness
preview: Problem: Our current admin utilities are a mixture of ad-hoc scripts, shell commands in runbooks, and multiple handcrafted Slack shortcuts. This causes knowledge silos, inconsistent execution, and poor auditability when support engineers perform common maintenance tasks. We frequently see follow-up bug churn when ma...
text:
Problem: Our current admin utilities are a mixture of ad-hoc scripts, shell commands in runbooks, and multiple handcrafted Slack shortcuts. This causes knowledge silos, inconsistent execution, and poor auditability when support engineers perform common maintenance tasks. We frequently see follow-up bug churn when manual steps are missed or parameters are wrong.

Goal: Deliver a lightweight operational template system and an automation harness that lets support and SRE staff run parameterized admin workflows as first-class tasks. Templates should: (1) be source-controlled (Git + templates directory), (2) expose safe default parameters with explicit confirmations, (3) create a discrete task instance that is auditable and trackable in the Ops dashboard, and (4) optionally wire into automated steps (scripts) or human-driven checkpoints.

Proposed solution: 3 components: 
1) Template schem...

title: Adaptive slot governor: simulating microbatch headroom and backpressure under sustained high QPS
preview: Problem: At sustained high QPS spikes we observe repeated short-lived GPU memory pressure events that cause aggressive KV cache evictions and cascading microbatch thrashing. Existing admission heuristics use fixed thresholds tuned to average load, which either under-utilize GPUs or cause elevated p99 latency and evi...
text:
Problem: At sustained high QPS spikes we observe repeated short-lived GPU memory pressure events that cause aggressive KV cache evictions and cascading microbatch thrashing. Existing admission heuristics use fixed thresholds tuned to average load, which either under-utilize GPUs or cause elevated p99 latency and eviction storms.

Goal: Implement an "adaptive slot governor" that continuously tunes microbatch admission thresholds and soft backpressure signals using a combination of short-window EWMA of queue depth, KV hit/evict rates, and a lightweight GPU headroom estimator. We will validate the governor in an offline simulator and a staged canary, instrumenting new telemetry to measure stability and cost impact.

Scope: This ticket covers design, simulator experiments, telemetry/alerting additions, and a staged runtime rollout strategy to test the governor's behavior under synthetic a...

title: Predictive turn-bundling coordinator with horizon-based KV prefetch for streaming chat
preview: Implement a coordinator that forms ephemeral bundles of streaming chat turns using a predictive horizon window. The coordinator will group turns from in-flight sessions into short-lived "bundles" when the model and KV cache predictions indicate overlapping KV slot access or contiguous token generation, enabling larg...
text: Implement a coordinator that forms ephemeral bundles of streaming chat turns using a predictive horizon window. The coordinator will group turns from in-flight sessions into short-lived "bundles" when the model and KV cache predictions indicate overlapping KV slot access or contiguous token generation, enabling larger continuous batches without adding tail latency for actively typing users. The approach combines a short predictive horizon (50-300ms), per-session priority scores, and a progressive KV prefetch strategy that warms only the most-likely KV slots to stay within per-GPU slab budgets.

title: Slate tile packer: adaptive tile-based batch formation with KV-tiling and GPU budget enforcement for streaming chat
preview: Problem: For streaming chat completions we need a batch formation strategy that minimizes tail latency while maximizing GPU utilization and respecting a strict per-GPU memory budget. Existing continuous-batching approaches either over-consolidate multi-turn sessions (increasing KV cache pressure) or under-bundle sho...
text: Problem: For streaming chat completions we need a batch formation strategy that minimizes tail latency while maximizing GPU utilization and respecting a strict per-GPU memory budget. Existing continuous-batching approaches either over-consolidate multi-turn sessions (increasing KV cache pressure) or under-bundle short-turn traffic (wasting kernel throughput). This ticket proposes a new 'slate tile packer' heuristic that treats incoming streaming requests as tiles placed into a bounded slate per worker, with explicit KV-tiling and GPU memory budgeting to avoid OOMs and excessive KV thrashing.

title: Python SDK: Structured model helpers and streaming ergonomics quickstart
preview: Goal: ship a small, opinionated set of helpers in the Python SDK that make it trivial to 1) declare typed response models (dataclass + optional Pydantic validation), 2) hydrate partial/streamed responses into typed objects as chunks arrive, and 3) provide ergonomic retry and backpressure hooks for streaming consumer...
text:
Goal: ship a small, opinionated set of helpers in the Python SDK that make it trivial to 1) declare typed response models (dataclass + optional Pydantic validation), 2) hydrate partial/streamed responses into typed objects as chunks arrive, and 3) provide ergonomic retry and backpressure hooks for streaming consumers. This ticket is the engineering carrier for an end-to-end quickstart that will be used in docs and the examples repo.

Background: we have many internal patterns across runtime and examples that parse streaming JSON or chunked 'delta' events into complete objects. Teams want a supported SDK surface that reduces boilerplate and avoids reimplementing fragile merge/hydration logic. This should integrate with existing Python SDK streaming iterators and respect retry semantics (idempotency, resumption), plus make it easy to opt into Pydantic for stricter validation when users ...

title: KV cache eviction regression & poison-susceptibility follow-up
preview: Summary: During a production capacity run on 2026-02-28 we observed repeated latency spikes and degraded throughput across several hosted clusters. Root cause analysis identified a compound failure mode in the KV/prefix cache: an eviction heuristic regression combined with an edge-case in cache key normalization all...
text:
Summary: During a production capacity run on 2026-02-28 we observed repeated latency spikes and degraded throughput across several hosted clusters. Root cause analysis identified a compound failure mode in the KV/prefix cache: an eviction heuristic regression combined with an edge-case in cache key normalization allowed stale/duplicated entries to remain referenced, causing memory growth and periodic cache poisoning (incorrect KV lookup results for hot prefixes). This ticket tracks follow-ups and action items from the postmortem aiming to fully remediate the regression, add guardrails, and close monitoring gaps.

What happened: A change in the eviction score calculation (PR #1984) altered LRU aging for small shard segments. Under typical workloads this increased cache churn, but under a specific prefix distribution we saw double-insert races where identical logical keys were stored wi...

title: Compact template-app onboarding: reference screenshots & README flow diagrams

Context:
- The docs team is introducing a condensed quickstart card for template apps used on the /quickstarts page. We need 6–8 annotated screenshots ...
text:
Goal: Produce a compact set of reference screenshots and a minimal README flow diagram for the Template Apps quickstart that fit within the new docs compact layout.

Context:
- The docs team is introducing a condensed quickstart card for template apps used on the /quickstarts page. We need 6–8 annotated screenshots (desktop + one mobile highlight) and a single-page README flow diagram that explains first-run behavior and environment variables.
- Target audience: developers trying Redwood Inference with a template app (Node + Next.js starter). Focus on clarity, minimal copy, and visual parity with existing console UI (rollouts, model selector, logs).

Deliverables (acceptance criteria):
1) 6 annotated reference screenshots exported at 1440px wide (PNG) and 640px wide (mobile crop) that include: 1) repo clone + .env checklist, 2) local dev server running with Redwood token input modal, ...

title: Improve table interaction parity and runtime performance
preview: Problem: Our table component has diverging behavior across density modes and struggles with large datasets. Users reported janky column-resize interactions, inconsistent keyboard focus when using virtualized rows, and confusing filter/sort affordances when density is compact. This ticket scopes a design and token up...
text:
Problem: Our table component has diverging behavior across density modes and struggles with large datasets. Users reported janky column-resize interactions, inconsistent keyboard focus when using virtualized rows, and confusing filter/sort affordances when density is compact. This ticket scopes a design and token update to deliver interaction parity across density modes, cleaner resize affordances, and layouts that enable engineering to implement efficient virtualization (variable row heights + sticky headers).

Goals:
- Establish consistent resize handles & hit targets across compact/comfortable/expanded density tokens.
- Define keyboard and screen-reader flow for column resize, reordering, and sorting that works with virtualization.
- Create visual and microinteraction specs for sorting and multi-column filters that scale to narrow column widths.
- Provide updated spacing/color/icon...

title: Welcome carousel + progressive disclosure for Dedicated capacity setup
preview: Design a compact entry experience for teams starting a Dedicated capacity trial that introduces key decisions (capacity sizing, autoscale policy, SLA tier) using a short welcome carousel and progressive disclosure panels. The flow should reduce cognitive load for first-time users by surfacing: a 3-card carousel that...
text: Design a compact entry experience for teams starting a Dedicated capacity trial that introduces key decisions (capacity sizing, autoscale policy, SLA tier) using a short welcome carousel and progressive disclosure panels. The flow should reduce cognitive load for first-time users by surfacing: a 3-card carousel that explains what Dedicated is, a capacity peek UI that visualizes throughput vs cost, an autoscale policy composer with example presets, and an SLA ribbon showing latency/uptime tradeoffs. Deliverables: high-fidelity screens for the console onboarding modal, a microcopy library for each panel, interactive prototype for user testing, and a small screenshot pack for release notes and docs.
